entities.name: DROUGHT
entities.description: A weather-related natural disaster characterized by a deficiency of precipitation over a season or an extended period of time

entities.name: FOOD PRODUCTION
entities.description: The process of producing food, which is negatively impacted by drought

entities.name: CROP PRODUCTION
entities.description: The process of growing crops, which decreases due to inadequate rainfall during drought

entities.name: RESILIENCE
entities.description: The ability of agricultural production to withstand environmental stress

entities.name: AGRICULTURAL PRODUCTION
entities.description: The overall production of agricultural goods under environmental stress

entities.name: INTERDROUGHT CONFERENCES
entities.description: A series of conferences serving as a platform for debating strategies to improve stress tolerance in crops

entities.name: DROUGHT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of crops to withstand drought stress

entities.name: STRESS TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of crops to withstand various environmental stresses

entities.name: CROPS
entities.description: Plants grown for food or other purposes that are the subject of improvement for stress tolerance

entities.name: CROP IMPROVEMENT
entities.description: The application of scientific and technological tools to enhance crop characteristics

entities.name: FRANCE
entities.description: The location where the first InterDrought Conference (ID-I) was held in 1995

entities.name: ITALY
entities.description: The location where the second InterDrought Conference (ID-II) was held in 2005

entities.name: CHINA
entities.description: The location where the third InterDrought Conference (ID-III) was held in 2009

entities.name: AUSTRALIA
entities.description: The location where the fourth InterDrought Conference (ID-IV) was held in 2013

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The location where the fifth InterDrought Conference (InterDrought-V) is being organized
